content: Tilapia is primarily farmed, with less than 5% coming from the United States, mostly from China or Latin America.
Tilapia is raised in small, confined areas, leading to high antibiotic usage in both water and food.
Antibiotics used in tilapia farming contribute to antibiotic resistance and disrupt gut microbiota.
Tilapia has the lowest omega-3 content of any fish, resulting in an omega-3 to omega-6 ratio that promotes inflammation.
Alternative white fish options include us-hack, California or Pacific halibut, and Alaskan cod.
Us-hack is sustainably caught, providing similar protein density and vitamin A levels to tilapia.
California or Pacific halibut has higher levels of vitamin A and a more balanced omega-3 to omega-6 ratio, making it an anti-inflammatory option.
Alaskan cod is lower on the food chain, reducing mercury absorption, and maintains its nutritional value when frozen.
